text.title: Financial Basics — Tracking Framework
text.summary: Four-stage financial tracking framework: Track, Analyze, Monitor, Review — with revenue categories, expense targets, and P&L structure.

rule.statement: Separate personal and business banking from day one. Never mix accounts. Open a dedicated business checking account and a dedicated tax-reserve savings account before accepting the first payment.
rule.rationale: Mixed accounts invalidate expense categorization automation, create tax deduction defensibility problems on audit, and make cash-flow analysis impossible. This is the single highest-leverage setup action.

rule.statement: Reconcile the books monthly within the first 5 days of the following month. Later than 5 days and the signal degrades — memory fades, receipts disappear, context is lost.
rule.rationale: Monthly reconciliation catches categorization errors while the founder still remembers the context of each transaction. Quarterly or annual reconciliation produces a noisy ledger that is hard to rely on for decisions.

list.item: Subscriptions: track MRR from Stripe dashboard or billing system — not from bank deposits (timing differs)
One-time sales: from payment processor exports
Services / consulting: from invoice system (track sent and paid separately)

rule.statement: Alert when any expense category exceeds its target percentage for two consecutive months. The alert is a flag for human review, not an automatic cut.
rule.rationale: Expense percentage drift is the earliest signal of profit margin erosion. Catching it in month two instead of year-end preserves the option to course-correct.

list.item: Infrastructure (hosting, tools, domains): target 5-15% of revenue
Marketing (ads, content, design tools): target 10-30% of revenue
Contractors (design, dev, support): target 10-30% of revenue
Owner pay: the remainder after expenses and tax reserve
Gross margin: (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue — target above 70% for SaaS
Operating margin: Operating profit / Revenue — target above 20%
Net margin: Net profit / Revenue — target above 10%
Rule of 40: Revenue growth % + Profit margin % — target above 40 for growth-stage SaaS
